1	import { useState, useEffect, useCallback, useRef } from 'react'
2	import {
3	  fetchCards,
4	  createCard as apiCreateCard,
5	  updateCard as apiUpdateCard,
6	  deleteCard as apiDeleteCard,
7	  createComment,
8	} from '../api/client.js'
9	
10	// ---------------------------------------------------------------------------
11	// Module-level helpers
12	// ---------------------------------------------------------------------------
13	
14	/**
15	 * Map API column value → state key.
16	 * 'in-progress' (hyphen) → 'in_progress' (underscore); others pass through.
17	 * @param {string} col
18	 * @returns {string}
19	 */
20	export function columnToKey(col) {
21	  return col === 'in-progress' ? 'in_progress' : col
22	}
23	
24	/**
25	 * Group a flat array of cards into the board state shape, sorted by position.
26	 * @param {import('../api/client.js').Card[]} cards
27	 * @returns {{ ready: Card[], in_progress: Card[], done: Card[] }}
28	 */
29	function groupCards(cards) {
30	  const board = { ready: [], in_progress: [], done: [] }
31	  for (const card of cards) {
32	    const key = columnToKey(card.column)
33	    if (key in board) board[key].push(card)
34	  }
35	  for (const key of Object.keys(board)) {
36	    board[key].sort((a, b) => a.position - b.position)
37	  }
38	  return board
39	}
40	
41	const EMPTY_BOARD = { ready: [], in_progress: [], done: [] }
42	
43	// Temp-ID counter for optimistic records (module-level so it's stable across renders)
44	let _tempId = 0
45	function nextTempId() {
46	  return '__temp_' + (++_tempId)
47	}
48	
49	// ---------------------------------------------------------------------------
50	// useBoard hook
51	// ---------------------------------------------------------------------------
52	
53	/**
54	 * Manages board state: cards grouped by column, loading, and error.
55	 *
56	 * Column convention
57	 *   - State keys:  ready | in_progress | done  (underscore)
58	 *   - API values:  ready | in-progress  | done  (hyphen for in-progress)
59	 *   - All hook functions that accept a column name expect the API format.
60	 *   - Each card's .column field retains the API format.
61	 *
62	 * @returns {{
63	 *   cards: { ready: Card[], in_progress: Card[], done: Card[] },
64	 *   loading: boolean,
65	 *   error: string|null,
66	 *   createCard: (data: object) => Promise<Card>,
67	 *   updateCard: (id: string, data: object) => Promise<Card>,
68	 *   deleteCard: (id: string) => Promise<void>,
69	 *   moveCard: (id: string, targetColumn: string, position?: number) => Promise<Card>,
70	 *   addComment: (cardId: string, data: object) => Promise<Comment>,
71	 * }}
72	 */
73	export function useBoard() {
74	  const [cards, setCards] = useState(EMPTY_BOARD)
75	  const [loading, setLoading] = useState(false)
76	  const [error, setError] = useState(null)
77	
78	  // Counter for concurrent in-flight operations so loading stays true
79	  // until the LAST operation finishes.
80	  const pendingRef = useRef(0)
81	
82	  // Ref that always holds the latest cards state so we can read it
83	  // synchronously (functional updaters are called lazily by React, which
84	  // would leave a rollback variable null if the API rejects as a microtask
85	  // before the updater runs).
86	  const cardsRef = useRef(EMPTY_BOARD)
87	
88	  /**
89	   * Apply a state update eagerly: compute the next value synchronously from
90	   * cardsRef.current, update the ref, then call setCards with the plain
91	   * computed value (not a function) so React picks it up on the next render.
92	   */
93	  function applyCards(updater) {
94	    const next = typeof updater === 'function' ? updater(cardsRef.current) : updater
95	    cardsRef.current = next
96	    setCards(next)
97	  }
98	
99	  function beginOp() { pendingRef.current++; setLoading(true) }
100	  function endOp()   { if (--pendingRef.current === 0) setLoading(false) }
101	
102	  // -------------------------------------------------------------------------
103	  // Initial data load
104	  // -------------------------------------------------------------------------
105	
106	  useEffect(() => {
107	    let cancelled = false
108	    setError(null)
109	    beginOp()
110	
111	    fetchCards()
112	      .then(data => {
113	        if (cancelled) return
114	        applyCards(groupCards(data))
115	        endOp()
116	      })
117	      .catch(err => {
118	        if (cancelled) return
119	        setError(err.message)
120	        endOp()
121	      })
122	
123	    return () => { cancelled = true }
124	  }, [])
125	
126	  // -------------------------------------------------------------------------
127	  // createCard — temp-ID optimistic pattern
128	  // -------------------------------------------------------------------------
129	
130	  const createCard = useCallback(async (data) => {
131	    const tempId = nextTempId()
132	    const col = data.column ?? 'ready'
133	    const key = columnToKey(col)
134	
135	    const optimisticCard = {
136	      id: tempId,
137	      title: data.title,
138	      assignee: data.assignee ?? null,
139	      column: col,
140	      position: Infinity,
141	      description: data.description ?? null,
142	      created_at: Date.now(),
143	      comments: [],
144	    }
145	
146	    applyCards(prev => ({ ...prev, [key]: [...prev[key], optimisticCard] }))
147	    beginOp()
148	
149	    try {
150	      const created = await apiCreateCard(data)
151	      const createdKey = columnToKey(created.column)
152	      applyCards(prev => {
153	        const next = { ...prev }
154	        // Remove the temp card from whichever column it was placed in
155	        next[key] = next[key].filter(c => c.id !== tempId)
156	        // Insert the server card into the column the server assigned
157	        next[createdKey] = [...next[createdKey], created]
158	          .sort((a, b) => a.position - b.position)
159	        return next
160	      })
161	      return created
162	    } catch (err) {
163	      applyCards(prev => ({
164	        ...prev,
165	        [key]: prev[key].filter(c => c.id !== tempId),
166	      }))
167	      throw err
168	    } finally {
169	      endOp()
170	    }
171	  }, [])
172	
173	  // -------------------------------------------------------------------------
174	  // updateCard — rollback pattern (column changes ignored; use moveCard)
175	  // -------------------------------------------------------------------------
176	
177	  const updateCard = useCallback(async (id, data) => {
178	    // Strip column — column changes must go through moveCard
179	    const safeData = { ...data }
180	    delete safeData.column
181	
182	    // Capture rollback synchronously from the ref (not inside a lazy updater)
183	    const rollback = cardsRef.current
184	    applyCards(prev => {
185	      const next = {}
186	      for (const [k, col] of Object.entries(prev)) {
187	        next[k] = col.map(c => c.id === id ? { ...c, ...safeData } : c)
188	      }
189	      return next
190	    })
191	    beginOp()
192	
193	    try {
194	      const updated = await apiUpdateCard(id, safeData)
195	      applyCards(prev => {
196	        const next = {}
197	        for (const [k, col] of Object.entries(prev)) {
198	          next[k] = col.map(c => c.id === id ? updated : c)
199	        }
200	        return next
201	      })
202	      return updated
203	    } catch (err) {
204	      applyCards(rollback)
205	      throw err
206	    } finally {
207	      endOp()
208	    }
209	  }, [])
210	
211	  // -------------------------------------------------------------------------
212	  // deleteCard — rollback pattern
213	  // -------------------------------------------------------------------------
214	
215	  const deleteCard = useCallback(async (id) => {
216	    // Capture rollback synchronously from the ref
217	    const rollback = cardsRef.current
218	    applyCards(prev => {
219	      const next = {}
220	      for (const [k, col] of Object.entries(prev)) {
221	        next[k] = col.filter(c => c.id !== id)
222	      }
223	      return next
224	    })
225	    beginOp()
226	
227	    try {
228	      await apiDeleteCard(id)
229	    } catch (err) {
230	      applyCards(rollback)
231	      throw err
232	    } finally {
233	      endOp()
234	    }
235	  }, [])
236	
237	  // -------------------------------------------------------------------------
238	  // moveCard — rollback pattern
239	  // targetColumn must be in API format ('ready' | 'in-progress' | 'done')
240	  // -------------------------------------------------------------------------
241	
242	  const moveCard = useCallback(async (id, targetColumn, position) => {
243	    const targetKey = columnToKey(targetColumn)
244	
245	    // Capture rollback synchronously from the ref
246	    const rollback = cardsRef.current
247	    applyCards(prev => {
248	      let cardToMove = null
249	      const next = {}
250	
251	      for (const [k, col] of Object.entries(prev)) {
252	        const idx = col.findIndex(c => c.id === id)
253	        if (idx !== -1) {
254	          cardToMove = col[idx]
255	          next[k] = col.filter(c => c.id !== id)
256	        } else {
257	          next[k] = col
258	        }
259	      }
260	
261	      if (!cardToMove) return prev
262	
263	      const movedCard = {
264	        ...cardToMove,
265	        column: targetColumn,
266	        ...(position !== undefined ? { position } : {}),
267	      }
268	      next[targetKey] = [...next[targetKey], movedCard]
269	        .sort((a, b) => a.position - b.position)
270	      return next
271	    })
272	    beginOp()
273	
274	    try {
275	      const payload = { column: targetColumn }
276	      if (position !== undefined) payload.position = position
277	      const updated = await apiUpdateCard(id, payload)
278	      const updatedKey = columnToKey(updated.column)
279	      applyCards(prev => {
280	        const next = {}
281	        for (const [k, col] of Object.entries(prev)) {
282	          next[k] = col.filter(c => c.id !== id)
283	        }
284	        next[updatedKey] = [...next[updatedKey], updated]
285	          .sort((a, b) => a.position - b.position)
286	        return next
287	      })
288	      return updated
289	    } catch (err) {
290	      applyCards(rollback)
291	      throw err
292	    } finally {
293	      endOp()
294	    }
295	  }, [])
296	
297	  // -------------------------------------------------------------------------
298	  // addComment — temp-ID optimistic pattern
299	  // -------------------------------------------------------------------------
300	
301	  const addComment = useCallback(async (cardId, data) => {
302	    const tempId = nextTempId()
303	    const optimisticComment = {
304	      id: tempId,
305	      card_id: cardId,
306	      author: data.author,
307	      content: data.content,
308	      created_at: Date.now(),
309	    }
310	
311	    applyCards(prev => {
312	      const next = {}
313	      for (const [k, col] of Object.entries(prev)) {
314	        next[k] = col.map(c =>
315	          c.id === cardId
316	            ? { ...c, comments: [...c.comments, optimisticComment] }
317	            : c
318	        )
319	      }
320	      return next
321	    })
322	    beginOp()
323	
324	    try {
325	      const comment = await createComment(cardId, data)
326	      applyCards(prev => {
327	        const next = {}
328	        for (const [k, col] of Object.entries(prev)) {
329	          next[k] = col.map(c =>
330	            c.id === cardId
331	              ? {
332	                  ...c,
333	                  comments: c.comments
334	                    .filter(cm => cm.id !== tempId)
335	                    .concat(comment),
336	                }
337	              : c
338	          )
339	        }
340	        return next
341	      })
342	      return comment
343	    } catch (err) {
344	      applyCards(prev => {
345	        const next = {}
346	        for (const [k, col] of Object.entries(prev)) {
347	          next[k] = col.map(c =>
348	            c.id === cardId
349	              ? { ...c, comments: c.comments.filter(cm => cm.id !== tempId) }
350	              : c
351	          )
352	        }
353	        return next
354	      })
355	      throw err
356	    } finally {
357	      endOp()
358	    }
359	  }, [])
360	
361	  // -------------------------------------------------------------------------
362	  // Public interface
363	  // -------------------------------------------------------------------------
364	
365	  return {
366	    cards,
367	    loading,
368	    error,
369	    createCard,
370	    updateCard,
371	    deleteCard,
372	    moveCard,
373	    addComment,
374	  }
375	}
376

Still writing error handling... Writing the comment form... I'm integrating the new CommentList component into CardModal by importing it and replacing the existing comments section, then threading the `onAddComment` handler through the component hierarchy from Board down to CardModal. I need to update the test file to reflect these changes as well. I need to update that timestamp test to verify the `dateTime` attributes on the `<time>` elements rather than checking exact text content, since the relative time formatting would return a full date string for timestamps over 30 days old. The existing CardModal tests should still pass after integrating CommentList since the DOM structure remains the same, but I'll need to fix the one test that explicitly checks the timestamp format.

For the CardModal tests, I should keep changes minimal—just fix the broken timestamp test and maybe add a smoke test to verify the comment form is integrated and connected. I also realized there's no existing `CardModal/` subdirectory under `client/src/components/`, so I'll need to create that directory structure as part of the refactoring.

# Task 15: Create CommentList Component ## Context The Kanban board application (`client/src/components/Board/CardModal.jsx`) already has a basic read-only comment display section (lines 185–208) using `toLocaleString()` for timestamps, but it has **no comment creation form** and timestamps are not in relative format. The `useBoard` hook already exposes `addComment(cardId, data)` (optimistic update + API call), but `Board.jsx` does not pass it to `CardModal`, and `CardModal` has no `onAddComment` prop. This task extracts the comment functionality into a new `CommentList` component in its own subdirectory, adds a comment creation form, and upgrades timestamps to relative format ("2 hours ago"). --- ## Files to Create | File | Purpose | |------|---------| | `client/src/components/CardModal/CommentList.test.jsx` | All CommentList unit tests (written first, TDD) | | `client/src/components/CardModal/CommentList.jsx` | Component implementation | | `client/src/components/CardModal/CommentList.css` | Component styles | ## Files to Modify | File | Change | |------|--------| | `client/src/components/Board/CardModal.jsx` | Add `onAddComment` prop; replace inline comment section with `` | | `client/src/components/Board/CardModal.test.jsx` | Fix the one timestamp test that will break (line 415–419) | | `client/src/components/Board/Board.jsx` | Destructure `addComment` from `useBoard()`; pass as `onAddComment` to `` | --- ## Subtask 1 — Comment Display (Red → Green → Refactor) ### 1a. Write tests first (`CommentList.test.jsx`) ```js import { render, screen, within } from '@testing-library/react' import { describe, it, expect, vi } from 'vitest' import CommentList, { formatRelativeTime } from './CommentList.jsx' ``` **Test cases for comment display:** - `renders a "Comments" heading` - `renders "No comments yet" when comments array is empty` - `does not render "No comments yet" when comments exist` - `renders each comment's author name` - `renders each comment's content text` - `renders comments in chronological order (oldest created_at appears first in DOM)` - `renders a element with ISO dateTime attribute for each comment` **Fixture:** ```js const comments = [ { id: 'cm1', card_id: '1', author: 'Bob', content: 'Looks good!', created_at: 1700000000000 }, { id: 'cm2', card_id: '1', author: 'Alice', content: 'Needs work', created_at: 1700000001000 }, ] ``` ### 1b. Implement `CommentList.jsx` (minimum to pass display tests) ```jsx export default function CommentList({ comments, onAddComment }) { return (

Comments

{comments.length === 0 ? (

No comments yet

) : (
    {comments.map(cm => (
  • {cm.author} {formatRelativeTime(cm.created_at)}

    {cm.content}

    • ))}
)} {/* form added in subtask 2 */}
) } ``` --- ## Subtask 2 — Comment Creation Form (Red → Green → Refactor) ### 2a. Add form tests to `CommentList.test.jsx` **Test cases for the creation form:** - `renders an author name input (aria-label "Author name")` - `renders a comment textarea (aria-label "Comment")` - `renders an "Add Comment" submit button` - `shows validation error "Author name is required" when submitting with empty author` - `shows validation error "Comment text is required" when submitting with empty content` - `calls onAddComment with { author, content } on valid submission` - `clears form fields after successful submission` - `disables submit button while onAddComment is pending` - `shows "Submitting…" text while pending` - `shows error message (role="alert") when onAddComment rejects` - `keeps form values when onAddComment rejects (does not clear)` ### 2b. Add form to `CommentList.jsx` ```jsx import { useState } from 'react' // inside CommentList: const [author, setAuthor] = useState('') const [content, setContent] = useState('') const [isSubmitting, setIsSubmitting] = useState(false) const [error, setError] = useState(null) async function handleSubmit(e) { e.preventDefault() if (author.trim() === '') { setError('Author name is required'); return } if (content.trim() === '') { setError('Comment text is required'); return } setIsSubmitting(true); setError(null) try { await onAddComment({ author: author.trim(), content: content.trim() }) setAuthor(''); setContent('') } catch (err) { setError(err.message) } finally { setIsSubmitting(false) } } ``` Form JSX (appended after the comment list): ```jsx
{error &&

{error}

} setAuthor(e.target.value)} disabled={isSubmitting} />

Here's a summary of what the plan covers:

## What Was Found

The codebase is a Kanban board app using **Vitest + React Testing Library**. Key facts:
- `CardModal.jsx` already has a **read-only** comment list using `toLocaleString()` — no creation form yet
- `useBoard.js` already has a working `addComment(cardId, data)` with optimistic updates, but **`Board.jsx` never passes it to `CardModal`**
- No `client/src/components/CardModal/` directory exists yet — needs to be created

## Plan Summary

**3 new files** + **3 modified files**, following strict TDD order:

1. **Write all tests first** (`CommentList.test.jsx`) covering: display ordering, empty state, creation form, validation, async submission, and 8 `formatRelativeTime` cases
2. **Implement `CommentList.jsx`** with exported `formatRelativeTime` utility and form state management
3. **Create `CommentList.css`** matching existing modal palette
4. **Update `CardModal.jsx`** — add `onAddComment` prop, replace inline comment section with `<CommentList>`
5. **Fix 1 breaking test** in `CardModal.test.jsx` (timestamp format changed from `toLocaleString()` to relative)
6. **Update `Board.jsx`** — destructure and pass `addComment` from `useBoard()` to `CardModal`

1	/**
2	 * API client for the Kanban board REST API.
3	 * All functions use relative URLs so Vite's dev proxy (/api → localhost:3001)
4	 * and same-origin production serving both work without configuration.
5	 */
6	
7	// ---------------------------------------------------------------------------
8	// Type definitions
9	// ---------------------------------------------------------------------------
10	
11	/**
12	 * @typedef {Object} Card
13	 * @property {string} id
14	 * @property {string} title
15	 * @property {string|null} assignee
16	 * @property {string} column - e.g. 'ready', 'in-progress', 'done'
17	 * @property {number} position
18	 * @property {string|null} description
19	 * @property {number} created_at - Unix ms timestamp
20	 * @property {Comment[]} comments
21	 */
22	
23	/**
24	 * @typedef {Object} Comment
25	 * @property {string} id
26	 * @property {string} card_id
27	 * @property {string} author
28	 * @property {string} content
29	 * @property {number} created_at
30	 */
31	
32	// ---------------------------------------------------------------------------
33	// ApiError
34	// ---------------------------------------------------------------------------
35	
36	export class ApiError extends Error {
37	  /**
38	   * @param {string} msg
39	   * @param {number} status  HTTP status code, or 0 for network-level failures
40	   * @param {*} data         Parsed error response body, or null
41	   */
42	  constructor(msg, status, data) {
43	    super(msg)
44	    this.name = 'ApiError'
45	    this.status = status
46	    this.data = data
47	  }
48	}
49	
50	// ---------------------------------------------------------------------------
51	// Internal fetch helper
52	// ---------------------------------------------------------------------------
53	
54	async function apiFetch(path, options = {}) {
55	  const headers = {}
56	  if (options.body !== undefined) headers['Content-Type'] = 'application/json'
57	
58	  let response
59	  try {
60	    response = await fetch(path, { ...options, headers: { ...headers, ...options.headers } })
61	  } catch (err) {
62	    throw new ApiError(`Network error: ${err.message}`, 0, null)
63	  }
64	
65	  if (!response.ok) {
66	    let errorData = null
67	    try { errorData = await response.json() } catch { /* ignore */ }
68	    const message = errorData?.error ?? `HTTP error ${response.status}`
69	    throw new ApiError(message, response.status, errorData)
70	  }
71	
72	  if (response.status === 204 || response.headers.get('content-length') === '0') {
73	    return null
74	  }
75	
76	  try {
77	    return await response.json()
78	  } catch (err) {
79	    throw new ApiError(`Failed to parse response: ${err.message}`, response.status, null)
80	  }
81	}
82	
83	// ---------------------------------------------------------------------------
84	// Public API functions
85	// ---------------------------------------------------------------------------
86	
87	/**
88	 * Fetch all cards with their nested comments.
89	 * @returns {Promise<Card[]>}
90	 * @throws {ApiError} on network or HTTP error
91	 * @example
92	 * const cards = await fetchCards()
93	 * console.log(cards[0].title)
94	 */
95	export async function fetchCards() {
96	  return apiFetch('/api/cards')
97	}
98	
99	/**
100	 * Create a new card.
101	 * @param {{ title: string, assignee?: string, column?: string, description?: string }} data
102	 * @returns {Promise<Card>}
103	 * @throws {ApiError} on network or HTTP error
104	 * @example
105	 * const card = await createCard({ title: 'Fix bug', assignee: 'Alice' })
106	 * console.log(card.id)
107	 */
108	export async function createCard(data) {
109	  return apiFetch('/api/cards', { method: 'POST', body: JSON.stringify(data) })
110	}
111	
112	/**
113	 * Partially update an existing card.
114	 * @param {string} id
115	 * @param {{ title?: string, assignee?: string, column?: string, description?: string, position?: number }} data
116	 * @returns {Promise<Card>}
117	 * @throws {ApiError} on network or HTTP error
118	 * @example
119	 * const updated = await updateCard('abc-123', { column: 'done' })
120	 */
121	export async function updateCard(id, data) {
122	  return apiFetch(`/api/cards/${id}`, { method: 'PATCH', body: JSON.stringify(data) })
123	}
124	
125	/**
126	 * Delete a card and all its comments.
127	 * @param {string} id
128	 * @returns {Promise<null>}
129	 * @throws {ApiError} on network or HTTP error
130	 * @example
131	 * await deleteCard('abc-123')
132	 */
133	export async function deleteCard(id) {
134	  return apiFetch(`/api/cards/${id}`, { method: 'DELETE' })
135	}
136	
137	/**
138	 * Add a comment to a card.
139	 * @param {string} cardId
140	 * @param {{ author: string, content: string }} data
141	 * @returns {Promise<Comment>}
142	 * @throws {ApiError} on network or HTTP error
143	 * @example
144	 * const comment = await createComment('abc-123', { author: 'Bob', content: 'Looks good!' })
145	 */
146	export async function createComment(cardId, data) {
147	  return apiFetch(`/api/cards/${cardId}/comments`, { method: 'POST', body: JSON.stringify(data) })
148	}

The test should verify that if comments somehow arrive out-of-order, they're still displayed correctly, though in practice the component will just render whatever order it receives since the data is already sorted upstream. I need to sort comments by `created_at` in the component and write a test that verifies this works even with out-of-order input. Looking at the concrete issues: the CSS import is missing from CommentList.jsx, the `formatRelativeTime` tests need actual test bodies with proper time mocking, the integration test needs a real fixture with expected values, and the orphaned CardModal.css styles won't be applied unless they're imported or the component structure changes. be dead code after migration. Plan should specify their removal.

5. **Comment sorting** — the plan says "renders comments in chronological order (oldest created_at appears first in DOM)" but doesn't specify the component should sort. The test fixture uses comments that are already in chronological order, so even without sorting the test would pass. The plan should either:
   a. Add explicit sorting in the component (defensive coding)
   b. OR explicitly state the test uses an out-of-order fixture to test sorting

Given the server already sorts and the hook maintains order, option (b) is less critical, but option (a) makes the component self-contained and robust. I'll add explicit sorting to the component and an out-of-order fixture test.

6. **`Board.test.jsx` note** — the plan doesn't mention that `Board.test.jsx` already has `addComment: vi.fn()` in `DEFAULT_STATE` and does NOT need modification. This is a clarifying note that prevents the LLM from either missing needed changes or making unnecessary ones.

Now I'm prioritizing these issues by severity: the missing CSS import is critical since styles won't load at all, the pseudocode tests and underspecified integration test are both important because they directly affect implementation quality, sorting is important since the task explicitly requires chronological order, while the dead CSS is just incomplete maintenance and the Board.test.jsx note is really just a clarification to avoid confusion.

The plan has been updated with the following corrections:

1. **Added `import './CommentList.css'`** explicitly in the implementation code.

2. **Replaced pseudocode tests with concrete test bodies** — 13 specific `formatRelativeTime` unit tests using `vi.useFakeTimers()` + `vi.setSystemTime(NOW)` with exact delta values and expected strings.

3. **Specified the integration timestamp test concretely** — freezes `Date.now()` at `NOW`, creates a comment at `NOW - 2h`, asserts `<time>` contains `"2 hours ago"`.

4. **Added comment sorting to the component** — `[...comments].sort((a, b) => a.created_at - b.created_at)` — and the corresponding ordering test now uses an out-of-order fixture to actually prove sorting works.

5. **Added `CardModal.css` cleanup** — explicitly lists all 9 orphaned selectors to remove (lines 128–178).

6. **Added `onAddComment` default value** — `= () => Promise.resolve()` to prevent crashes in tests or production contexts where the prop is omitted.

7. **Clarified `Board.test.jsx` status** — explicitly states it doesn't need modification since it already contains `addComment: vi.fn()` in `DEFAULT_STATE`.

content.text:
I found three genuine issues:

1. **Execution order contradicts TDD instructions**: The execution order says "write ALL tests first, implement everything at once" but the task's TDD instructions require per-subtask red-green cycles. The stub in Subtask 1b only makes sense in an incremental flow — this contradiction will cause the implementing LLM to do it wrong.

2. **Subtask 1 `<time>` test is vague**: The plan describes the test in prose ("check `dateTime` attribute") but the fixture has **2 comments**, so the test needs to check both. Without concrete code (e.g., `within(commentItems[0]).getByRole('time')`), the LLM will likely only check one, or write a fragile `document.querySelectorAll`.

3. **Integration timestamp test leaks fake timers on assertion failure**: The test calls `vi.useFakeTimers()` inside the test body and `vi.useRealTimers()` at the end — if the assertion throws, real timers are never restored, corrupting subsequent tests. The fix is to move it into the `formatRelativeTime` describe block where `beforeEach`/`afterEach` already manage timer lifecycle safely.

content.text:
I've carefully re-read the entire plan looking for any remaining correctness or completeness issues. Here is my assessment:

- **Test logic is sound**: The chronological ordering test correctly supplies an out-of-order fixture and asserts position 0 = older comment. ✓
- **`getByRole('time')` is valid**: RTL 16 uses aria-query 5.x which maps `<time>` to the `time` ARIA role. Both `within(items[0]).getByRole('time')` and `screen.getByRole('time')` will work correctly. ✓
- **Fake timer scoping is correct**: The integration render test is inside the `formatRelativeTime` describe, so `beforeEach`/`afterEach` cleanly manage timer lifecycle. ✓
- **Import path** `'../CardModal/CommentList.jsx'` from `Board/CardModal.jsx` resolves correctly to `components/CardModal/CommentList.jsx`. ✓
- **Stub returns `''`**: The Subtask 1 display test only checks the `dateTime` attribute, not text content — the stub passes Subtask 1 cleanly and the Subtask 3 integration test correctly fails (red) with the stub before passing (green) with the real implementation. ✓
- **`onAddComment` default prop**: Prevents crashes in the ~40 existing CardModal test renders that don't supply the prop. ✓
- **`Board.test.jsx`** already has `addComment: vi.fn()` in `DEFAULT_STATE` — confirmed no changes needed. ✓
- **CSS orphan removal** and **execution order per-subtask TDD cycle** are both correctly specified. ✓

---

## Summary

**3 new files created:**

- **`CommentList.test.jsx`** — 32 tests covering display, form, and timestamp formatting
- **`CommentList.jsx`** — Component with:
  - Chronological sort of comments (oldest first)
  - Empty state ("No comments yet")
  - `<time>` elements with ISO `dateTime` attributes
  - Author name input + comment textarea form with validation
  - Loading state ("Submitting…"), error display, form clearing on success
  - `formatRelativeTime()` — relative timestamps (just now → minutes/hours/days ago → locale date)
- **`CommentList.css`** — Full styling for comment threads and the creation form

**4 existing files updated:**

- **`CardModal.jsx`** — Added `onAddComment` prop (defaults to no-op), replaced inline comment section with `<CommentList>`
- **`CardModal.css`** — Removed 9 orphaned `.modal-comments*` selectors
- **`CardModal.test.jsx`** — Replaced the `toLocaleString()` timestamp test with ISO `dateTime` attribute check; updated comment describe renders with `onAddComment={vi.fn()}`
- **`Board.jsx`** — Destructured `addComment` from `useBoard()` and wired it through to `CardModal` as `onAddComment`

This session is being continued from a previous conversation that ran out of context. The summary below covers the earlier portion of the conversation. Summary: 1. Primary Request and Intent: The user requested implementation of Task 15: "Create CommentList Component" for a Kanban board application. The task involved: - Creating `client/src/components/CardModal/CommentList.jsx` showing a chronological list of comments with author names and timestamps - Adding a form for creating new comments with author name input and comment textarea - Formatting timestamps in relative format (e.g., "2 hours ago") - Adding proper CSS styling for comment threads - Handling the empty comment state - Following strict TDD (red → green → refactor per subtask) - Running `update-subtask-status` after each subtask After implementation, the user asked to "Carefully evaluate the current implementation" by checking requirements against the implementation using `git status`, `git diff main`, reading key files, running tests, and running lint — then fixing all identified issues. 2. Key Technical Concepts: - React functional components with hooks (`useState`, `useEffect`, `useRef`) - Vitest 2.0.5 + React Testing Library 16.0.0 (testing framework) - Per-subtask TDD: write tests first (red), implement (green), refactor - `vi.useFakeTimers()` / `vi.setSystemTime()` for controlling `Date.now()` in tests - `within()` scoped queries in RTL - Optimistic updates pattern in `useBoard` hook - `createPortal` for modal rendering - ESLint with `react-hooks/rules-of-hooks` rule active - Relative timestamp formatting logic (seconds → minutes → hours → days → locale date) - `aria-label` for accessible form inputs - `role="alert"` for validation/error messages - `role="time"` for `` HTML elements in RTL queries - CSS: `.comment-list*` and `.comment-form*` class naming 3. Files and Code Sections: - **`kanban/client/src/components/CardModal/CommentList.jsx`** (NEW — main deliverable) - Exports `formatRelativeTime(timestamp)` function and default `CommentList` component - Sorts comments chronologically (`[...comments].sort((a, b) => a.created_at - b.created_at)`) - Renders "No comments yet" empty state, comment list with `` elements, and creation form - Form has author name input, comment textarea, submit button with loading/error states ```jsx import { useState } from 'react' import './CommentList.css' export function formatRelativeTime(timestamp) { const diff = Date.now() - timestamp const seconds = Math.floor(diff / 1000) const minutes = Math.floor(seconds / 60) const hours = Math.floor(minutes / 60) const days = Math.floor(hours / 24) if (seconds < 60) return 'just now' if (minutes < 60) return minutes === 1 ? '1 minute ago' : `${minutes} minutes ago` if (hours < 24) return hours === 1 ? '1 hour ago' : `${hours} hours ago` if (days === 1) return 'yesterday' if (days < 30) return `${days} days ago` return new Date(timestamp).toLocaleDateString() } export default function CommentList({ comments, onAddComment }) { const sorted = [...comments].sort((a, b) => a.created_at - b.created_at) const [author, setAuthor] = useState('') const [content, setContent] = useState('') const [isSubmitting, setIsSubmitting] = useState(false) const [error, setError] = useState(null) async function handleSubmit(e) { e.preventDefault() if (author.trim() === '') { setError('Author name is required'); return } if (content.trim() === '') { setError('Comment text is required'); return } setIsSubmitting(true); setError(null) try { await onAddComment({ author: author.trim(), content: content.trim() }) setAuthor(''); setContent('') } catch (err) { setError(err.message) } finally { setIsSubmitting(false) } } return (

Comments

{sorted.length === 0 ? (

No comments yet

) : (
    {sorted.map(cm => (
  • {cm.author} {formatRelativeTime(cm.created_at)}

    {cm.content}

    • ))}
)}
{error &&

{error}

} setAuthor(e.target.value)} disabled={isSubmitting} />

1	import { render, screen, fireEvent, waitFor, within } from '@testing-library/react'

4

8	  assignee: 'Alice',

11	  position: 1,
12	  created_at: Date.now(),
13	  comments: [],
14	}
15

25	  })

29	    expect(screen.getByRole('dialog')).toHaveTextContent('Alice')
30	  })
31

33	    render(<CardModal card={{ ...card, assignee: null }} onClose={vi.fn()} onUpdate={vi.fn()} onDelete={vi.fn()} />)
34	    expect(screen.getByRole('dialog')).toHaveTextContent('Unassigned')
35	  })
36

72	    expect(handler).not.toHaveBeenCalled()
73	  })
74	})
75	
76	describe('CardModal — portal', () => {

78	    const { container } = render(<CardModal card={card} onClose={vi.fn()} onUpdate={vi.fn()} onDelete={vi.fn()} />)
79	    // Portal renders into document.body, NOT inside the React render container
